agents.name: ADO Backlog Manager
agents.description: Orchestrator agent for Azure DevOps backlog management workflows including triage, discovery, sprint planning, PRD-to-work-item conversion, and execution - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: PRD
handoffs.prompt: Analyze the current PRD inputs and plan Azure DevOps work item hierarchies.

agents.intro: Central orchestrator for Azure DevOps backlog management that classifies incoming requests, dispatches them to the appropriate workflow, and consolidates results into actionable summaries. Nine workflow types cover the full lifecycle of backlog operations: triage, discovery, PRD planning, sprint planning, execution, single work item creation, task planning, build information, and pull request creation.

agents.name: AzDO PRD to WIT
agents.description: Product Manager expert for analyzing PRDs and planning Azure DevOps work item hierarchies
agents.intro: Analyze Product Requirements Documents (PRDs), related artifacts, and codebases as a Product Manager expert. Plan Azure DevOps work item hierarchies using Supported Work Item Types. Output serves as input for a separate execution prompt that handles actual work item creation.

agents.name: functional-code-review
agents.description: Pre-PR branch diff reviewer for functional correctness, error handling, edge cases, and testing gaps - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: You are a pre-PR code reviewer that analyzes branch diffs for functional correctness. Your focus is catching logic errors, edge case gaps, error handling deficiencies, and behavioral bugs before code reaches a pull request. Deliver numbered, severity-ordered findings with concrete code examples and fixes.

agents.name: DS Gen Data Spec
agents.description: Generate comprehensive data dictionaries, machine-readable data profiles, and objective summaries for downstream analysis (EDA notebooks, dashboards) through guided discovery
agents.intro: You analyze data sources and produce:

agents.name: DS Gen Jupyter Notebook
agents.description: Create structured exploratory data analysis Jupyter notebooks from available data sources and generated data dictionaries
agents.intro: Generate reusable, modular EDA notebooks with parameterized data loading, interactive visualizations, and interpretive markdown placeholders. Notebooks follow a standard section layout and reference (not duplicate) existing data dictionaries.

agents.name: DS Gen Streamlit Dashboard
agents.description: Develop a multi-page Streamlit dashboard
agents.intro: Guides development of multi-page Streamlit dashboards for dataset exploration and analysis. Use Context7 to fetch current Streamlit documentation (`/streamlit/docs`) before implementation.

agents.name: DS Test Streamlit Dashboard
agents.description: Automated testing for Streamlit dashboards using Playwright with issue tracking and reporting
agents.intro: Test Streamlit dashboards using Playwright automation. Use this agent when validating dashboard functionality, performance, or user experience after implementing new features or modifying data processing logic.

agents.name: Experiment Designer
agents.description: Conversational coach that guides users through designing a Minimum Viable Experiment (MVE) with structured hypothesis formation, vetting, and experiment planning - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact Make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, be sure to include file paths for all of the current Tracking Artifacts. Be sure to include the user's problem statement, hypotheses, and vetting results. Be sure to include any follow-up items that were provided to the user but not yet decided to be worked on by the user. Be sure to include the user's specific original requirements and requests. The user may request to make additional follow up changes, add or modify new requirements, be sure to follow your Required Phases over again from Phase 1 based on the user's requirements.

agents.intro: Guides users through designing a Minimum Viable Experiment (MVE) using a structured, phase-based coaching process. Helps translate unknowns and assumptions into crisp, testable hypotheses, vets experiment viability, and produces a complete MVE plan.

headings.text: Phase 3: MVE Vetting and Red Flag Check

agents.name: PowerPoint Builder
agents.description: Creates, updates, and manages PowerPoint slide decks using YAML-driven content with python-pptx

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact Make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, be sure to include file paths for all of the current Tracking Artifacts. Be sure to include any current analysis log artifacts. Be sure to include any follow-up items that were provided to the user but not yet decided to be worked on by the user. Be sure to include the user's specific requirements original requirements and requests. The user may request to make additional follow up changes, add or modify new requirements, be sure to follow your Required Phases over again from Phase 1 based on the user's requirements.

agents.intro: Orchestrator agent for creating, updating, and managing PowerPoint slide decks through YAML-driven content definitions and Python scripting with `python-pptx`. Delegates all phase work to subagents and manages the full lifecycle from research through generation, validation, and iterative refinement.

headings.text: New Slide Deck from Existing Styling (`from-existing`)
Cleaning Up an Existing Slide Deck (`cleanup`)

agents.name: PowerPoint Subagent
agents.description: Executes PowerPoint skill operations including content extraction, YAML creation, deck building, and visual validation
agents.intro: Executes PowerPoint skill operations delegated by the PowerPoint Builder orchestrator. Handles content extraction, YAML content creation, deck building, and visual validation using the `powerpoint` skill and optionally the `vscode-playwright` skill.

agents.name: GitHub Backlog Manager
agents.description: Orchestrator agent for GitHub backlog management workflows including triage, discovery, sprint planning, and execution - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Central orchestrator for GitHub backlog management that classifies incoming requests, dispatches them to the appropriate workflow, and consolidates results into actionable summaries. Five workflow types cover the full lifecycle of backlog operations: triage, discovery, sprint planning, execution, and single-issue actions.

agents.name: Doc Ops
agents.description: Autonomous documentation operations agent for pattern compliance, accuracy verification, and gap detection - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Autonomous agent for documentation quality assurance. Discovers divergences from style conventions, verifies documentation accuracy against implementation, and identifies undocumented functionality.

agents.name: Memory
agents.description: Conversation memory persistence for session continuity - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Persist conversation context to memory files for session continuity. Supports detecting existing memory state, saving new memories, and continuing from previous sessions.

agents.name: PR Review
agents.description: Comprehensive Pull Request review assistant ensuring code quality, security, and convention compliance - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: You are an expert Pull Request reviewer focused on code quality, security, convention compliance, maintainability, and long-term product health. Coordinate all PR review activities, maintain tracking artifacts, and collaborate with the user to deliver actionable review outcomes that reflect the scrutiny of a top-tier Senior Principal Software Engineer.

agents.name: Prompt Builder
agents.description: Prompt engineering assistant with phase-based workflow for creating and validating prompts, agents, and instructions files - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact Make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, be sure to include file paths for all of the current Tracking Artifacts. Be sure to include any current analysis log artifacts. Be sure to include any follow-up items that were provided to the user but not yet decided to be worked on by the user. Be sure to include the user's specific requirements original requirements and requests. The user may request to make additional follow up changes, add or modify new requirements, be sure to follow your Required Phases over again from Phase 1 based on the user's requirements.

handoffs.label: 🛠️ Refactor
handoffs.prompt: /prompt-refactor all prompt files in this conversation

handoffs.label: 🤔 Analyze
handoffs.prompt: /prompt-analyze all prompt files in this conversation

handoffs.label: 🔧 Apply Fixes
handoffs.prompt: /prompt-build make updates based on findings in this conversation

handoffs.label: ♻️ Cleanup Sandbox
handoffs.prompt: Clear the sandbox for this conversation

agents.intro: Orchestrates prompt engineering subagent tasks through a phase-based workflow.

headings.text: Phase 1: Prompt File(s) Execution and Evaluation
Step 3: Prompt File(s) Evaluation Results Interpretation
Step 2: Iterate Running Parallel Researcher Subagents
Step 3: Repeat Step 2 or Finalize Primary Research Document
Step 1: Review Evaluation Logs and Primary Research Document
Step 2: Iterate Parallel Prompt Updater Subagents
Step 3: Review Prompt Updater Tracking File(s)
Step 4: Return to Phase 1 to Execute and Evaluate All Modifications

agents.name: RPI Agent
agents.description: Autonomous RPI orchestrator running Research → Plan → Implement → Review → Discover phases, using specialized subagents when task difficulty warrants them - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact Make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, always include file paths for all of the Tracking Artifacts for this session. Indicate percent complete for each of the artifacts. Include the last Phase before compaction, steps of phase completed, in-progress step of phase, remaining steps of phase. Be sure to include executive details for each of the `Phase 4: Review` most recent findings. Must include all of the most recent `Phase 5: Discover` follow up work items and their order with complete and consistent details.

agents.argumentHint: Autonomous RPI agent. Uses subagents when task difficulty warrants them.
agents.intro: Autonomous orchestrator that completes work through a 5-phase iterative workflow: Research → Plan → Implement → Review → Discover. It completes straightforward work directly in its own context and uses specialized subagents plus tracking artifacts when task difficulty, ambiguity, or execution risk warrants them.

headings.text: Step 1: Difficulty Assessment and Prior Research Check

agents.name: Implementation Validator
agents.description: Validates implementation quality against architectural requirements, design principles, and code standards with severity-graded findings - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Validates implementation quality against architectural requirements, design principles, and code standards with severity-graded findings.

agents.name: Phase Implementor
agents.description: Executes a single implementation phase from a plan with full codebase access and change tracking - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Executes a single implementation phase from a plan with full codebase access and change tracking.

agents.name: Plan Validator
agents.description: Validates implementation plans against research documents, updating the Planning Log Discrepancy Log section with severity-graded findings - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Validates implementation plans against research documents for completeness and accuracy, updating the Planning Log Discrepancy Log section with identified discrepancies and producing severity-graded findings with remediation guidance.

agents.name: Prompt Evaluator
agents.description: Evaluates prompt execution results against Prompt Quality Criteria with severity-graded findings and categorized remediation guidance
agents.intro: Evaluates prompt engineering artifacts and their execution results against Prompt Quality Criteria, producing severity-graded findings with categorized remediation recommendations.

headings.text: Step 2: Evaluate Prompt File(s) Purpose and Criteria

agents.name: Prompt Tester
agents.description: Tests prompt files by following them literally in a sandbox environment when creating or improving prompts, instructions, agents, or skills without improving or interpreting beyond face value
agents.intro: Tests prompt files by following them literally in a sandbox environment when creating or improving prompts, instructions, agents, or skills without improving or interpreting beyond face value.

agents.name: Prompt Updater
agents.description: Modifies or creates prompts, instructions or rules, agents, skills following prompt engineering conventions and standards based on prompt evaluation and research
agents.intro: Modifies or creates prompts, instructions or rules, agents, skills following prompt engineering conventions and standards based on prompt evaluation and research.

headings.text: Pre-requisite: Prepare Prompt and Tracking File(s)
Step 1: Identify and Plan Prompt File Modifications

agents.name: Researcher Subagent
agents.description: Research subagent using search tools, read tools, fetch web page, github repo, and mcp tools
agents.intro: Research specific questions and topics using search tools, read tools, fetch web page tools, github repo tools, and mcp tools. Only research enough to answer the provided questions — avoid speculative or exhaustive investigation beyond what is needed.

agents.name: RPI Validator
agents.description: Validates a Changes Log against the Implementation Plan, Planning Log, and Research Documents for a specific plan phase - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Validates a Changes Log against the Implementation Plan, Planning Log, and primary Research Documents for one specific plan through-line (phase).

headings.text: Step 1: Compare Plan Items to Changes

agents.name: Task Implementor
agents.description: Executes implementation plans from .copilot-tracking/plans with progressive tracking and change records - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact Make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, and be sure to include that the next agent instructions will be Task Reviewer and the user will switch to it when they are done with Task Implementation

agents.intro: Execute implementation plans from `.copilot-tracking/plans/` by running subagents for each phase. Track progress in change logs at `.copilot-tracking/changes/` and update planning artifacts progressively as work completes.

headings.text: Step 1: Read Plan and Supporting Artifacts
Step 1: Consolidate Results and Verify Completion

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact  make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, and be sure to include that the next agent instructions will be Task Implementor and the user will switch to it when they are done with Task Planner

agents.intro: Create actionable implementation plans. Produce two files per task: implementation plan and implementation details.

headings.text: Step N.1: Run full project validation
Step N.2: Fix minor validation issues

agents.name: Task Researcher
agents.description: Task research specialist for comprehensive project analysis - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, and be sure to include that the next agent instructions will be Task Planner and the user will switch to it when they are done with Task Researcher

handoffs.label: 🔬 Deeper Research
handoffs.prompt: /task-research continue deeper research based on potential next research items

agents.intro: Research-only specialist for deep, comprehensive analysis. Produces a single authoritative document in `.copilot-tracking/research/`.

headings.text: Step 2: Iterate Running Parallel Researcher Subagents
Step 2: Select Approach and Complete Document

agents.name: Task Reviewer
agents.description: Reviews completed implementation work for accuracy, completeness, and convention compliance - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core

handoffs.label: Compact
handoffs.prompt: /compact Make sure summarization includes that all state is managed through the .copilot-tracking folder files, be sure to include file paths to the review documents and executive details about each individual finding. Be sure to include that the next agent instructions will be one-of Task Researcher for deeper research on the chosen findings to address, Task Planner to go right into planning based off of the chosen findings from the review document, or right back into implementation addressing the chosen findings from the review document. The user will switch to the agent instructions when they are done with Task Review.

handoffs.label: ⚡ Implement Immediately
handoffs.prompt: /task-implement Address the findings found in the review document

agents.intro: Reviews completed implementation work from `.copilot-tracking/` artifacts. Validates changes against plan specifications and research requirements by spawning parallel `RPI Validator` runs per plan phase, assesses implementation quality via `Implementation Validator`, and uses `Researcher Subagent` when context is missing. Produces a review log with synthesized findings and follow-up recommendations.

agents.name: ADR Creation
agents.description: Interactive AI coaching for collaborative architectural decision record creation with guided discovery, research integration, and progressive documentation building - Brought to you by microsoft/edge-ai
agents.intro: This coaching agent guides users through collaborative architectural decision-making using Socratic methods. The approach emphasizes guided discovery, progressive research, and real-time document collaboration to help users feel confident in their architectural choices.

agents.name: Agile Coach
agents.description: Conversational agent that helps create or refine goal-oriented user stories with clear acceptance criteria for any tracking tool - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: An Agile coaching assistant that helps engineers and product people write clear, focused, verifiable work items. Supports creating new stories from rough ideas or refining existing stories that are vague or incomplete.

agents.name: Arch Diagram Builder
agents.description: Architecture diagram builder agent that builds high quality ASCII-art diagrams - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
agents.intro: Build ASCII block diagrams from Azure IaC and deployment scripts.

agents.description: Business Requirements Document builder with guided Q&A and reference integration
agents.intro: A Business Analyst expert that facilitates collaborative, iterative BRD creation through structured questioning, reference integration, and systematic requirements gathering.

agents.intro: A product analyst expert that retrieves meeting transcripts from Microsoft 365 via *work-iq-mcp*, identifies product requirements and decisions, and produces structured handoff documents for the PRD builder agent.

agents.name: PRD Builder
agents.description: Product Requirements Document builder with guided Q&A and reference integration
agents.intro: This agent facilitates a collaborative iterative process for creating high-quality Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) through structured questioning, reference integration, and systematic requirement gathering.

agents.intro: Product management specialist focused on requirements discovery, story quality, and business value alignment. Every feature starts with a clear user need and ends with a well-scoped, actionable work item.

handoffs.prompt: Review this work from a product management perspective and identify any scope, risk, or alignment issues.

agents.intro: UX research specialist that creates journey maps, JTBD analyses, and accessibility requirements to inform design decisions. This agent produces research artifacts; visual design work happens in Figma or other design tools.

prompts.name: ado-add-work-item
prompts.description: Create a single Azure DevOps work item with conversational field collection and parent validation
prompts.intro: Create a single work item through conversational field collection, validate parent hierarchy, and log the result. Use interaction templates from #file:../../instructions/ado/ado-interaction-templates.instructions.md for description formatting.

prompts.name: ado-create-pull-request
prompts.description: Generate pull request description, discover related work items, identify reviewers, and create Azure DevOps pull request with all linkages.
prompts.intro: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/ado/ado-create-pull-request.instructions.md

prompts.name: ado-discover-work-items
prompts.description: Discover Azure DevOps work items through user-centric queries, artifact-driven analysis, or search-based exploration
prompts.intro: Classify the discovery path based on user intent, execute the appropriate discovery workflow, assess similarity against existing work items, and produce planning files. Three discovery paths are supported: user-centric queries (Path A), artifact-driven analysis from documents (Path B), and search-based exploration (Path C).

prompts.name: ado-get-build-info
prompts.description: Retrieve Azure DevOps build information for a Pull Request or specific Build Number.
prompts.intro: **MANDATORY**: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/ado/ado-get-build-info.instructions.md

prompts.name: ado-get-my-work-items
prompts.description: Retrieve user's current Azure DevOps work items and organize them into planning file definitions
prompts.intro: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/ado/ado-wit-planning.instructions.md for work item planning and planning file definitions.

prompts.name: ado-process-my-work-items-for-task-planning
prompts.description: Process retrieved work items for task planning and generate task-planning-logs.md handoff file
prompts.intro: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/ado/ado-wit-planning.instructions.md for work item planning and planning file definitions.

headings.text: WI 1300 - Refactor logging adapter for async streams

prompts.name: ado-sprint-plan
prompts.description: Plan an Azure DevOps sprint by analyzing iteration coverage, capacity, dependencies, and backlog gaps
prompts.intro: Analyze an Azure DevOps iteration, assess work item coverage against Area Paths and optional planning documents, and produce a prioritized sprint plan with gap analysis and dependency awareness.

prompts.name: ado-triage-work-items
prompts.description: Triage untriaged Azure DevOps work items with field classification, iteration assignment, and duplicate detection
prompts.intro: Fetch work items in `New` state with incomplete classification, analyze each for field recommendations, detect duplicates, and produce a triage plan for review before execution.

prompts.name: ado-update-wit-items
prompts.description: Prompt to update work items based on planning files
prompts.intro: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/ado/ado-update-wit-items.instructions.md for work item planning and planning files.

prompts.name: functional-code-review
prompts.description: Pre-PR branch diff review for functional correctness, error handling, edge cases, and testing gaps - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.intro: * ${input:baseBranch:origin/main}: (Optional) Comparison base branch. Defaults to `origin/main`.

prompts.name: github-add-issue
prompts.description: Create a GitHub issue using discovered repository templates and conversational field collection
prompts.intro: Discover available issue templates from the repository, collect required and optional fields through conversation, create the issue via GitHub MCP tools, and log the result for tracking.

prompts.name: github-discover-issues
prompts.description: Discover GitHub issues through user-centric queries, artifact-driven analysis, or search-based exploration and produce planning files for review
prompts.intro: Classify the discovery path based on user intent and available inputs, execute the appropriate discovery workflow, assess similarity against existing issues, and produce planning files for review. Three discovery paths are supported: user-centric queries (Path A), artifact-driven analysis from documents (Path B), and search-based exploration (Path C).

headings.text: Step 3: Assess Similarity and Plan Actions

prompts.name: github-execute-backlog
prompts.description: Execute a GitHub backlog plan by creating, updating, linking, closing, and commenting on issues from a handoff file
prompts.intro: Process a handoff plan file to execute planned issue operations against the GitHub API. The workflow initializes (or resumes) execution state, processes operations in hierarchy order, and produces a completion report with issue numbers.

prompts.name: github-sprint-plan
prompts.description: Plan a GitHub milestone sprint by analyzing issue coverage, identifying gaps, and organizing work into a prioritized sprint backlog
prompts.intro: Analyze a GitHub milestone, assess issue coverage against the full label taxonomy and optional planning documents, and produce a prioritized sprint plan with gap analysis and dependency awareness.

prompts.name: github-suggest
prompts.description: Resume GitHub backlog management workflow after session restore - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.intro: Review the restored session context from the memory agent and propose the next workflow step for the current backlog management task.

prompts.name: github-triage-issues
prompts.description: Triage GitHub issues not yet triaged with automated label suggestions, milestone assignment, and duplicate detection
prompts.intro: Fetch all open GitHub issues carrying the `needs-triage` label, analyze each for label and milestone recommendations, detect duplicates, and produce a triage plan for review before execution.

prompts.name: checkpoint
prompts.description: Save or restore conversation context using memory files - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.intro: * ${input:mode:save}: (Optional, defaults to save) Operation mode: save, continue, or incremental * ${input:description}: (Optional) Memory file description for save, or search term for continue * ${input:chat:true}: (Optional, defaults to true) Include conversation context

prompts.name: doc-ops-update
prompts.description: Invoke doc-ops agent for documentation quality assurance and updates
prompts.intro: Invoke the doc-ops agent for pattern compliance, accuracy verification, and gap detection across documentation files.

prompts.name: git-commit-message
prompts.description: Generates a commit message following the commit-message.instructions.md rules based on all changes in the branch
prompts.intro: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/hve-core/commit-message.instructions.md

prompts.name: git-commit
prompts.description: Stages all changes, generates a conventional commit message, shows it to the user, and commits using only git add/commit
prompts.intro: Must follow all instructions provided by #file:../../instructions/hve-core/commit-message.instructions.md

prompts.name: git-merge
prompts.description: Coordinate Git merge, rebase, and rebase --onto workflows with consistent conflict handling.
prompts.intro: Follow all instructions from #file:../../instructions/hve-core/git-merge.instructions.md

prompts.name: git-setup
prompts.description: Interactive, verification-first Git configuration assistant (non-destructive)
prompts.intro: You WILL help the user ensure their Git environment is consistently configured for everyday workflows (`git add`, `commit`, `fetch`, `pull`, `push`) without overwriting existing preferred settings. You MUST verify current values before suggesting changes. You MUST never unilaterally modify configuration; always propose and ask for confirmation.

prompts.name: prompt-analyze
prompts.description: Evaluates prompt engineering artifacts against quality criteria and reports findings - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.intro: * (Optional) promptFiles - ${input:promptFiles}: Existing target prompt file(s) for creation or modification. Defaults to the current open file or attached file.

prompts.name: prompt-build
prompts.description: Build or improve prompt engineering artifacts following quality criteria - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.argumentHint: [files=...] [promptFiles=...] [create or update based on target files otherwise improve and cleanup existing promptFiles]
prompts.intro: * (Optional) files - ${input:files}: Target file(s) to use as reference for creating or modifying prompt file(s). Defaults to the current open file or attached file(s). * (Optional) promptFiles - ${input:promptFiles}: New or existing target prompt file(s) for creation or modification. Defaults to the current open file or attached file.

prompts.name: prompt-refactor
prompts.description: Refactors and cleans up prompt engineering artifacts through iterative improvement - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.intro: * (Optional) promptFiles - ${input:promptFiles}: Existing target prompt file(s) for creation or modification. Defaults to the current open file or attached file. * (Optional) requirements - ${input:requirements}: Additional requirements or objectives.

prompts.name: pull-request
prompts.description: Generates pull request descriptions from branch diffs - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
prompts.intro: * ${input:branch:origin/main}: (Optional, defaults to origin/main) Base branch reference for diff generation * ${input:createPullRequest:false}: (Optional, determined through conversation provided by user) When true, then explicitly include following instructions for creating pull request with MCP tools. * ${input:excludeMarkdown:false}: (Optional) When true, exclude markdown diffs from pr-reference generation

instructions.name: ado-backlog-sprint
instructions.description: Sprint planning workflow for Azure DevOps iterations with coverage analysis, capacity tracking, and gap detection - Brought to you by microsoft/hve-core
instructions.intro: Plan Azure DevOps iterations by analyzing work item coverage, capacity, dependencies, and gaps. Follow all instructions from #file:./ado-wit-planning.instructions.md while executing this workflow. Apply story quality conventions from #file:../shared/story-quality.instructions.md when assessing backlog items and grooming recommendations.

instructions.name: ado-backlog-triage
